basic_details.job_title: Oil India Contractual Field Assistant Recruitment 2026 - Walkin
basic_details.short_information: Oil India has published the Contractual Field Assistant (Land) Notification 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill 5 Contractual Field Assistant (Land) positions. Candidates within the age range of 25–40 years (Gen), 25–43 years (OBC-NCL), and 25–45 years (ST) and holding a 10th-pass qualification with Qualified Recorder's Certificate Class Course (RCCC) or equivalent are eligible to attend the walk-in personal assessment at the official venue. The application process will be active on 28 May 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and details through the direct links bel

age_limit.age_details: Minimum: 25 years, Maximum: Gen: 40 years, OBC-NCL: 43 Years, ST: 45 Years
age_limit.details: Minimum Age: 25 years Maximum Age: 40 years (General) Age Relaxation: OBC-NCL: 43 Years ST: 45 Years

qualification.details: Contractual Field Assistant (Land): Must possess Qualified Recorder's Certificate Class Course (RCCC) of Lot Mandal/ Land Record Assistance from Government Department OR Must have passed HSLC examination or equivalent examination from any Government Recognized Board. Must have minimum 10 (ten) years work experience in Govt./PSU/Public/Private Sector organization of repute, engaged in land acquisition, including but not limited to land survey/land acquisition and revenue matters including liaison with revenue authorities.

salary_details.pay_scale: Fixed: ₹ 21,450.00 per month, Variable: ₹ 825.00 per day
salary_details.details: Fixed Emolument: ₹ 21,450.00 per month (Based on attendance including paid leave, holiday, if any.) Variable Emolument: ₹ 825.00 per day (For each working day.)

selection_process: Walk-in for Personal Assessment(s) of total 100 marks Qualifying/pass marks: 50 for all categories Assessment on Professional Knowledge and skills
Personal Attributes and Soft Skills Final selection based on merit

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to attend the walk-in interview for Contractual Field Assistant (Land) posts?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview for the Contractual Field Assistant (Land) posts is scheduled for 28th May 2026. Candidates should report for registration between 07:00 A.M. to 09:00 A.M. on this date.

faq.question: Q2. What is the minimum qualification required for the Contractual Field Assistant (Land) position?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed the HSLC examination or equivalent from a government-recognized board, or possess a Qualified Recorder's Certificate Class Course (RCCC) from a government department.

faq.question: Q3. How many years of experience are required for this role?
faq.answer: A minimum of 10 years of work experience in government, PSU, public, or private sector organizations engaged in land acquisition, land survey, or revenue matters is required.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for the Contractual Field Assistant (Land) posts?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 25 years, and the maximum age for General candidates is 40 years. Age relaxation is applicable for OBC-NCL (43 years) and ST (45 years).

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary structure for the Contractual Field Assistant (Land) role?
faq.answer: The salary includes a fixed emolument of ₹ 21,450.00 per month and a variable emolument of ₹ 825.00 per day for each working day.

faq.question: Q6. How many vacancies are available for the Contractual Field Assistant (Land) post?
faq.answer: There are a total of 5 vacancies for the Contractual Field Assistant (Land) position.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for these positions?
faq.answer: The selection process involves a walk-in for personal assessment, which includes evaluation of professional knowledge, skills, personal attributes, and soft skills. Final selection will be based on merit.

faq.question: Q8. Where will the walk-in interview be conducted?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview will be conducted at the Oil India office in Duliajan, Assam. Candidates should report to the venue for registration.

faq.question: Q9. What documents should candidates bring to the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must bring all original documents and self-attested copies, including ID proof, educational certificates, experience certificates, and category certificates (if applicable).

faq.question: Q10. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification for this recruitment drive.
